Paths/Frontend Development/Next.js Fundamentals

Next.js Fundamentals

Learn server-side rendering, routing, and full-stack React with Next.js.

Next.js is a React framework that adds server-side rendering, routing, and API routes out of the box.

Key Concepts

  • File-based Routing: Pages map to files in the `app/` directory.
  • Server Components: Components that render on the server by default.
  • Client Components: Add `'use client'` for interactive components.
  • API Routes: Build backend endpoints in `app/api/`.
  • Layouts: Shared UI that wraps multiple pages.

Example

// app/page.tsx - Server Component
export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <h1>Welcome to my Next.js app</h1>
    </main>
  );
}

// app/api/hello/route.ts - API Route
export async function GET() {
  return Response.json({ message: 'Hello!' });
}

Resources
